name: Oreads
description: Oreads are nymphs of Greek mythology, specifically mountain nymphs. They are often associated with particular mountains or groves and are considered daughters of Zeus or sometimes Oceanus and Gaia, depending on the source. Oreads are generally depicted as beautiful, free-spirited women who delight in the wilderness and the company of wild animals. They are known for their connection to nature, their ability to influence the weather on their mountains, and their protective nature towards the flora and fauna within their domain. Oreads are immortal or have extremely long lifespans, and they are often involved in the lives of mortals, sometimes as lovers, sometimes as guides, and sometimes as protectors. Their presence is said to bring fertility and abundance to the land. While generally benevolent, they could be vengeful if their mountains or forests were desecrated. They are often depicted as companions of Artemis, the goddess of the hunt, and are skilled hunters themselves. They are also associated with Dionysus, the god of wine and revelry, and often participate in his ecstatic rites. Oreads are a vital part of the Greek mythological landscape, representing the untamed beauty and power of the natural world.
subtitle: Mountain Nymphs of Greek Lore
abilities: Enhanced speed and agility in mountainous terrain
Healing properties related to mountain herbs
appearance: Oreads typically appear as beautiful young women with features reflecting the mountains they inhabit. They often have long, flowing hair the color of earth or moss, and eyes that sparkle like mountain streams. Their skin can be tanned from the sun and wind, and they often wear simple, natural clothing made from leaves, flowers, or animal hides. They are often adorned with wildflowers and may carry a staff or bow. Their movements are graceful and swift, like a mountain goat leaping across the rocks.
habitat: Oreads reside in mountains, forests, caves, and grottoes of Greece. Each Oread is typically associated with a specific mountain or mountain range and considers it her home and domain. They are most often found in secluded, untouched areas of the wilderness.
behavior: Oreads are generally benevolent and enjoy the company of mortals who respect nature. They are playful and love to dance and sing in the mountains. They are fiercely protective of their homes and will defend them against those who would despoil them. They can be unpredictable and easily angered by disrespect towards the natural world. They are often solitary creatures, but they also gather in groups for celebrations and rituals.
weaknesses: Deforestation and destruction of their habitat
Disrespect towards nature and the gods

keyMyths.title: Echo and Narcissus
keyMyths.description: The Oread Echo fell in love with Narcissus, but he rejected her. Heartbroken, she wasted away until only her voice remained, forever echoing in the mountains. This myth highlights the tragic consequences of unrequited love and the Oreads' connection to the natural world.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: Contains stories involving nymphs, including Echo.
sources.text: Contains stories involving nymphs, including Echo.
